_about: Inputs for E2 (competing-attraction toy model) verification. SI constants drive the physical toy model (proton + two electrons). Geometry: proton at origin, electron E1 at +d, electron E2 at +d+r (both on the same side).

geometry_asymmetric._about: Config A — proton at origin, both electrons on +x side. The load-bearing config for sign-correct apparent repulsion.

x_E1.source: choose d = Bohr radius (natural length scale)

x_E2.source: E2 offset from E1 by r

geometry_symmetric_control._about: Config B — proton at origin between the electrons; control case. Expected: apparent *attraction*, opposite sign.

reference_values._provenance_note: Values re-computed 2026-04-24 with CODATA-precision a_0 = 5.29177210903e-11 m. Session C derivation.md used truncated a_0 = 5.29e-11 m, which shifts all absolute values by ~0.07% (the ratio is unchanged). Higher-precision values below are verified by both Python/mpmath@30dps and Wolfram Engine to 15+ digits agreement.

F_E1_from_proton_expected.source: Recomputed 2026-04-24 with CODATA a_0; Session C derivation.md had -3.634473e-47 with truncated a_0

ddot_r_expected.source: a_E2 - a_E1; recomputed 2026-04-24; Session C had 2.988141e-17

F_effective_expected.source: F_eff = (m_e/2) * ddot_r; recomputed 2026-04-24; Session C had 1.360949e-47

F_Coulomb_at_a0.source: k_C e^2 / a_0^2; recomputed 2026-04-24; Session C had 8.242838e-08

ratio_F_competing_Coulomb.source: F_effective / F_Coulomb; matches Session C to rel. err 1.0e-7 (ratio is insensitive to the a_0 truncation since it cancels)
